work.name: Aghaeepour Lab | Stanford Biomedical Data Science
work.summary: Preparing multimodal data pipelines to support pre-training and fine-tuning of RL-based foundation models for clinical reasoning and neonatal patient trajectory prediction. Supervised by Dr. Weijieying Ren.

work.name: Vázquez-García Lab | Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
work.summary: Supervised by Prof. Ignacio Vázquez-García (Broad Institute, Massachusetts General Hospital, Harvard Medical School). Deploying single-cell genomics data analysis pipelines to study tumor clonal evolution.
highlights: Analyzing and integrating electronic health records to support multimodal patient representation learning and patient trajectory prediction in genomically unstable cancers.

work.name: Rabinovitch Lab | Stanford University School of Medicine
work.position: Paid Bioinformatics and Cardiovascular Research Intern
work.summary: Applied bioinformatics, transcriptomics, imaging informatics, and functional assays to investigate how loss-of-function mutations can cause the pathogenesis of pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) and other rare cardiovascular diseases.
highlights: Developed a computational framework to analyze RNA-seq data from co-cultures of wild-type and knockout stem cell-derived endothelial and smooth muscle cells; optimized co-cultures for experimental consistency.
Investigated the role of TBX4 loss in driving endothelial-like gene expression in smooth muscle cells, with broader contributions to research on promoter optimization for haploinsufficiency research.
Selected as the first high school student to participate in the Single-Cell Omics Analysis Jamboree hosted by the IGVF Consortium.

work.name: Lynbrook High School
work.summary: Developing machine learning algorithms using electronic health records (EHRs) and medical imaging to predict adverse clinical events.
highlights: Developing supervised, weakly supervised, self-supervised, and unsupervised machine learning and deep learning models for medical image analysis and clinical natural language processing.

Grand Prize Winner of the Synopsys Silicon Valley Science and Technology Championship and 1st Grand Award and Top Award winner at the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair. Named a 2025 Regeneron Science Talent Search Scholar.

education.area: Biological Engineering, minor in Computer Science

awards.title: MIT Huang-Hobbs Innovation Award
awards.awarder: MIT Biological and Chemical Engineering Faculty and Broad Institute Core Faculty
awards.summary: Awarded Grand Prize for research at the MIT BioMakers: From Bench to Body Symposium.

awards.title: Dudley R. Herschbach SIYSS Top Award
awards.awarder: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF)
awards.summary: Awarded 1 of 12 best-in-fair Top Awards out of 1,657 finalists and 10 million global entries at the 2025 Regeneron ISEF.

awards.title: First Grand Award and Best of Category in Translational Medical Science at ISEF '25
awards.awarder: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF)
awards.summary: Selected as Best of Category in Translational Medical Science worldwide for research discovering a low-cost, non-invasive, text-based digital biomarker for pan-cancer prognosis prediction via free-text clinical narratives.

awards.title: 2025 Regeneron Science Talent Search (STS) Scholar
awards.summary: Named a Top-300 Scholar in the nation's oldest and most prestigious science and mathematics research competition for high school seniors.

awards.title: 2nd Place Prize by Silicon Valley Engineering Council
awards.summary: $3,000 cash prize dedicated to promoting STEM innovation.

awards.title: Grand Prize Winner at Synopsys Silicon Valley Science and Technology Championship
awards.awarder: Synopsys Silicon Valley Science and Technology Championship
awards.summary: Selected to compete at the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) as a finalist from the Santa Clara Valley Science and Engineering Fair.

projects.name: Discovery of a Novel Digital Biomarker for Early Pan-Cancer Survival and Recurrence Prediction via Free-Text Clinical Narratives
projects.summary: Used natural language processing to assess the use of free-text clinical narratives in fitting survival and recurrence outcomes for pan-cancer patients at initial diagnosis, enabling risk stratification across 32 cancer types. Earned top awards at 2025 International Science and Engineering Fair. Named a Top-300 Scholar in the 2025 Regeneron Science Talent Search (STS).

projects.name: Investigating the Role of TBX4 Loss-of-Function in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH)
projects.summary: Developed an automated RNA-sequencing differential expression and functional analysis application, using co-culture models of stem-cell differentiated wild-type and mutated endothelial and smooth muscle cells, to study how TBX4 loss-of-function mutations can lead to a PAH phenotype.
